run_scope_note: Phase A (S1-S6) shipped 2026-07-06. Phase B (S7-S9) shipped 2026-07-07 after ink published visuals-v0.1.2. v1 complete per spec.md Definition of Done. v2 remains out of scope.
codex_unavailable_note: Codex out of credits at S1 review gate 2026-07-05; user authorized Opus-only review for the remainder of that run. Still out of credits at S7 review gate 2026-07-06 (Phase B); user re-authorized Opus-only review for the remainder of this Phase B run too. Still out of credits at the final whole-tree pre-commit gate 2026-07-07; covered by the same standing authorization.

manual_validation.evidence: S8 (2026-07-06): resolved via a real Chromium browser (Playwright's own headless build, NOT the sandbox's Playwright-MCP tool — that tool's chrome-for-testing/chrome channels could not be made to work in this Linux ARM64 sandbox; see MV-2 evidence for detail). Added tests/e2e/banner-decor.spec.ts 'AC-UX-6/MV-1: aquarelle nav banner swaps to a freshly-generated SVG that differs across two separate page loads' — loads / with aquarelle active twice (two full page loads, not just two mounts), reads getComputedStyle(el).backgroundImage on [data-testid=nav-banner-decor] both times, and asserts (a) both are data:image/svg+xml URIs and (b) the two differ. Ran against a real `next dev` server: passed standalone (13/13 in the file, repeated twice) and as part of the full non-relay e2e bucket (63/63 via `node scripts/run-e2e.mjs`). This proves the boxProps/style split renders correctly in a real DOM (Chakra does not silently drop the data-URI backgroundImage) and that the real @rotheric/visuals engine's per-load non-determinism reaches the browser, not just the pure resolveDynamicBannerStyle function the jsdom-less unit suite exercises.

S8 (2026-07-06): resolved via the same real Chromium browser path as MV-1 (the sandbox's Playwright-MCP tool could not be used — its bundled chrome-for-testing channel downloaded successfully via `npx @playwright/mcp install-browser chrome-for-testing` but the MCP server kept reporting it as not installed across repeated retries, and the alternate 'chrome' channel failed outright with 'not supported on Linux Arm64'; both are sandbox/tooling limitations, not something fixable from within this session without an MCP server restart the available tools do not expose). Added tests/e2e/banner-decor.spec.ts 'MV-2: rapidly toggling live between aquarelle and a static-only theme never leaves a broken/blank banner or logs an uncaught error' — drives the REAL in-SPA setTheme() toggle path (via the /profile theme-picker buttons, not page.goto reloads) rapidly between aquarelle and calm 5 times, then settles on aquarelle and asserts: (a) the final nav-banner-decor background is always a valid, non-blank data:image/svg+xml URI (AC-UX-3a's fail-soft contract, holds even under rapid supersession), (b) zero uncaught pageerror events, (c) zero console.error messages naming worker/banner/generat*/dynamicVisuals machinery. Ran green standalone and as part of the full non-relay bucket. This is a practical proxy for 'no stale SVG ever applied and no Worker threads accumulate' — Playwright cannot directly enumerate live Worker threads or introspect the cancelled-flag guard's internal state, so this real-browser check corroborates (does not replace) the structural source-read verification architecture.md's Boundary Rule 7 note already relies on.

make build succeeds under output:export; self.addEventListener (worker side effect) appears exactly once, in the isolated chunk out/_next/static/chunks/6865.e5beb5c9dc6fac5a.js, and zero times in the main _app/main chunks. Independently re-verified twice this session (once by the lead directly via grep, once by the Stage-1 Opus reviewer via its own make build + chunk inspection). User-authorized 2026-07-06.

verified by 3-way verification-examiner pass on S7 (VQ-S7-001/002/003/004/007/009 all YES, confidence 0.95-1.0): real @rotheric/visuals import replaces the stub, signature/call-sites byte-identical, single-import-site invariant re-targeted and holds, seam preserved for S8. Opus code-review clean after 1 remediation round (Codex unavailable, user-authorized Opus-only).

verified by verification-examiner pass on S7 (VQ-S7-006/008/012 all YES, confidence 1.0): make build under output:export succeeds, banner.worker.ts consumes the real package unchanged, grep of built chunks confirms zero sharp/Node-only deps in the client bundle, engine code appears exactly once (dedup'd worker chunk, absent from _app).

verification-examiner pass on S8 (VQ-S8-002/003/004/007/010/012 YES or waived-per-Decider): brand-new light theme, zero shared-file edits beyond S1, auto-detected by folder-driven registry, frozen static fallback genuinely captured from real @rotheric/visuals output (re-verified non-degenerate after Decider-directed remediation).

verification-examiner pass on S8 (VQ-S8-008/013 YES/PARTIAL-accepted): passes real WCAG contrast gate with margin; ships status:experimental; oversized static banner is a disclosed, S9-scoped condition now tracked via inline TODO comment.

verification-examiner pass on S8 (VQ-S8-009/014 YES): 13/13 real-browser e2e tests green post-remediation, including hardened MV-1/MV-2 poll-based non-determinism and no-broken-banner checks against the re-frozen banner.

S9 (2026-07-06): render lite-preset knobs finalized ({ zones: 2, layers: 3, darkening: 1, halo: 0, splatter: 0, smoothness: 28 }, derived from reading tools/watercolor/svg.js's element-emission logic and confirmed over 30 real draws) — fixes <path> count at exactly 6 with zero <circle> elements, byte size 8.4-9.6 KB, inside the ~6-path/~8-15 KB target. aquarelle/manifest.ts's treatments.dynamic.banner.render updated; frozen static treatments.banner fallback re-captured at these exact knobs (8567 chars, replacing S8's oversized ~54.7KB no-render-override capture). Perf measurement: (a) real @rotheric/visuals renderSVG Node.js execution time at this preset, p50=0.11ms/p99=0.23ms over 200 real calls (3x faster than the old unbounded preset's p50=0.35ms/p99=1.15ms); (b) real headless-Chromium check (project's own @playwright/test install, not the sandbox's non-functional Playwright-MCP tool) under CDP CPU throttling at 1x and 4x (Lighthouse's standard low-end-mobile multiplier) showed the banner already swapped to a fresh generated SVG by the first post-navigation read at both rates, with zero main-thread longtask entries attributable to generation (consistent with off-thread Worker execution, structurally verified in S3/S5 and real-browser-confirmed for aquarelle by S8's MV-1/MV-2); (c) real-browser CLS for aquarelle (0.0046) sits at/below lego's (0.0066, a theme with a custom webfont but no dynamic banner), isolating the small measured CLS to ordinary font-loading rather than the banner swap — both far below the 0.1 'good' CWV threshold. Honest limitation (see S9 result.json): this sandbox has no reachable physical low-end mobile device; the 4x CDP throttle is a documented proxy per Lighthouse's own methodology, not a real-device measurement. status flipped 'experimental' -> 'stable' on this evidence. New regression-guard test added (app/tests/unit/themes/treatments.test.ts, 'S9 regression guard (AC-PERF-3)') asserting the manifest's actual render knobs keep real output within this band going forward — a mechanical safety net, not a substitute for this measurement. Architect-direct-invocation run (no lead-driven Stage-1/Stage-2 review in this session — see S9 result.json); full unit suite green (2068/2068, baseline 2068 includes S9's own 3 new tests over the 2065 S8 baseline), typecheck clean.

ac_closure_scope_note: Phase A (20 ACs) + Phase B (AC-DEP-1/2, AC-THEME-1/2, AC-UX-6, AC-PERF-3 - 6 ACs) now fully closed, 26/26 in-scope ACs met. v2 (AC-THEME-3, AC-STRUCT-7) remains explicitly out of scope per spec.md Amendments.

gate_runs.detail: Phase B re-run (2026-07-06): 3 modules with real logic audited (useDynamicBanner.ts, dynamicVisuals.ts, schema.ts) - 2 real gaps found and closed (empty-static-banner passthrough test; StyleToken scheme enum missing 2/6 round-trip cases), both re-verified killed. Layout.tsx/aquarelle manifest.ts/registry.generated.ts correctly skipped by established convention (no jsdom harness / pure data / generated file). Supersedes Phase A's tool_failure (Stryker timeouts) residual.

Phase B re-run (2026-07-07), full 48-test-class gate via make test-e2e-all: fast bucket 63 passed (1.9m); groups/relay bucket 216 passed, 3 skipped (pre-existing, unrelated to this epic - profile-request/reactive/member-profiles tests), 0 failed (25.9m). Includes S8 aquarelle MV-1/MV-2 real-browser tests, all green. Supersedes Phase A skip (no live UI surface then - aquarelle now ships).
